name: Israel Adesanya

qualityWinLedger.context: UFC champion rematch win, but limited UFC sample at the time. Approved division/timing calibration.
Repeat win over elite champion-level middleweight.
Elite interim-title war opponent at middleweight.
Elite middleweight title challenger, age/activity context.
All-time name, but late-career timing heavily discounted.
Quality middleweight win on contender climb.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Jan was upward-division, Pereira was avenged, and Strickland did not close it because Izzy returned to title-level. DDP is endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Adesanya avenged the loss by knockout in the immediate rematch.
Returned directly to title-level competition against Dricus du Plessis.

lossContextEvents.type: prime upward-division elite title decision loss

lossContextEvents.context: Occurred after the Dricus du Plessis prime endpoint; penalty is zero.

shortCase: Adesanya is the modern middleweight title-volume case: elite striking, repeated defenses, two wins over Whittaker, and a long run as the face of the division.
bestArgument: Adesanya's case starts with middleweight title volume: repeated defenses, two wins over Whittaker, the Pereira revenge moment, and years as the face of the division.
counterArgument: The counterargument is that the later losses and Pereira rivalry make the case less clean than the longest-reigning champions above him.
whyRankedHere: Adesanya ranks here because his middleweight championship volume is real: title win, repeated defenses, a title regain, and a deep list of modern contenders.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because the later losses make the case less clean, and the Pereira/Strickland/DDP stretch creates prime-dominance drag. The title volume is excellent, but the resume is not as clean as the names above him.
finalTakeaway: Adesanya is the modern middleweight title-volume standard: deep, active, and important, but less clean than the longest-reign GOAT cases above him.
